# Agentic Coding for Educators

> A hands-on course teaching college and university faculty and academic librarians —
> with no programming experience — to build and ship their own web tools using AI
> (Claude artifacts → GitHub → Vercel → Claude Code). Fourteen modules across three
> levels, from a first page in Claude to three live builds: a quiz app, a flashcard
> study site, and a full course portal. Responsible building (privacy, accessibility,
> FERPA, IRB, IP ownership, campus policy) is built in throughout.

Site: https://learn.brusovcoach.org
Audience: higher-ed faculty and academic librarians (academic higher-ed only; not K-12 or public libraries).
Tracks: Faculty and Academic Librarian — same modules, worked examples tuned to each.
Modules are sequential (1 → 14); each has a hands-on exercise and a 5-question quiz.

## Level 1 — Foundations
- You Can Build Software Now: /module/you-can-build-software-now
- Meet Your AI Builder: /module/meet-your-ai-builder
- How to Talk to AI: Prompting for Builders: /module/talking-to-ai-prompting
- What Is a Website, Really?: /module/what-is-a-website
- Plan Before You Build: /module/plan-before-you-build

## Level 2 — Applied
- Your Toolbox: Claude, GitHub, Vercel: /module/your-toolbox
- Build #1: A Class Quiz App: /module/build-quiz-app
- Getting It Online: /module/getting-it-online
- Fixing and Changing Things: /module/fixing-and-changing
- Build #2: A Flashcard Study Site: /module/build-flashcard-site

## Level 3 — Advanced
- Meet Claude Code: /module/meet-claude-code
- Build #3: A Full Course Portal: /module/build-course-portal
- Building Responsibly: Data, Privacy, Accessibility, Ownership & Policy: /module/building-responsibly
- Good Habits, Costs & Your Capstone: /module/habits-costs-capstone

## Pages
- Full curriculum: /curriculum
- Levels: /level/foundations, /level/applied, /level/advanced
- About: /about
